study_type: Randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, multicenter, two-part immunotherapy trial (pembrolizumab vs placebo)
visit_structure: Part 1: blinded treatment phase until first disease recurrence; Part 2: cross-over or re-challenge with pembrolizumab after unblinding at first recurrence. EOT visit at 30 days +/- 2 weeks after discontinuation. Imaging every 12 weeks in Part 2. Data entry required within 5 days of each patient visit.

field_definitions.institution_number: Unique identifier for the participating institution
field_definitions.step_number: Registration step number (Step 1 = registration, Step 2 = randomization)
field_definitions.eligibility_confirmed: Confirmation that all eligibility criteria have been checked one by one
field_definitions.adverse_events: All adverse events graded per NCI CTCAE v4.0
field_definitions.concomitant_medications: All concomitant medications recorded at each visit
field_definitions.wbc: White blood cell count (complete blood count)
field_definitions.pregnancy_test: Pregnancy test performed at EOT visit
field_definitions.urine_analysis: Urine dipstick (specific gravity, pH, proteins, glucose, blood); microscopic if needed
field_definitions.hrqol: Health-related quality of life assessed via EORTC QLQ-C30 v3 and EQ-5D-3L questionnaires

validation_rules.data_entry_window: All data must be entered in the eCRF within 5 days of each patient visit
validation_rules.recurrence_data_entry: Upon first recurrence, all pending data must be entered within 1 business day or before site unblinding
validation_rules.sae_reporting: All SAEs/ECIs must be reported to EORTC within 24 hours of the event
validation_rules.sar_reporting: SARs for patients on investigator's choice of treatment must be reported to the MAH of the administered drug
validation_rules.ae_grading: All AEs graded using NCI CTCAE v4.0
validation_rules.dili_eci: Drug Induced Liver Injury (DILI): AST or ALT >= 3x ULN with concurrent total bilirubin >= 2x ULN and alkaline phosphatase < 2x ULN — report as ECI
validation_rules.pneumonitis_eci: Pneumonitis/interstitial lung disease: report as ECI if >= Grade 2
validation_rules.nephritis_eci: Nephritis/renal failure: report as ECI if >= Grade 2
validation_rules.colitis_eci: Colitis and related GI events: report as ECI if >= Grade 2 or any grade resulting in dose modification or systemic steroids
validation_rules.endocrine_eci: Endocrine events: report as ECI if >= Grade 3 or >= Grade 2 resulting in dose modification or systemic steroids
validation_rules.hematologic_eci: Hematologic events: report as ECI if >= Grade 3 or any grade resulting in dose modification or systemic steroids
validation_rules.hepatic_eci: Hepatic events: report as ECI if >= Grade 2 or any grade resulting in dose modification or systemic steroids
validation_rules.neurologic_eci: Neurologic events (autoimmune neuropathy, Guillain-Barre, demyelinating polyneuropathy, myasthenic syndrome): report as ECI regardless of grade
validation_rules.ocular_eci: Uveitis/Iritis: report as ECI if >= Grade 2 or any grade resulting in dose modification or systemic steroids
validation_rules.skin_severe_eci: Dermatitis exfoliative, erythema multiforme, Stevens-Johnson syndrome, toxic epidermal necrolysis: report as ECI regardless of grade
validation_rules.skin_grade3_eci: Pruritus, rash, generalized rash, maculo-papular rash: report as ECI if >= Grade 3
validation_rules.infusion_reaction_eci: Allergic reaction, anaphylaxis, cytokine release syndrome, serum sickness, infusion reactions: report as ECI regardless of grade
validation_rules.other_eci: Myocarditis, pancreatitis, pericarditis: report as ECI regardless of grade; any other Grade 3 immune-related event
validation_rules.creatinine_eci: Creatinine elevations: report as ECI if >= Grade 3 or any grade resulting in dose modification or systemic steroids
validation_rules.tsh_reflex: If TSH is elevated, add free T3 and free T4 testing
validation_rules.imaging_baseline: All baseline evaluations must be performed no more than 4 weeks before beginning of treatment
validation_rules.measurable_lesion: Single measurable lesion >= 10 mm in two dimensions; multiple measurable lesions >= 10 mm in at least one dimension on spiral CT
validation_rules.equipment_calibration: All critical equipment (laboratory, imaging, drug administration) must be suitably calibrated and maintained; documentation retained as source documentation

ast.note: ECI threshold: >= 3x ULN concurrent with total bilirubin >= 2x ULN and ALP < 2x ULN (DILI)

alt.note: ECI threshold: >= 3x ULN concurrent with total bilirubin >= 2x ULN and ALP < 2x ULN (DILI)

total_bilirubin.note: ECI threshold: >= 2x ULN concurrent with AST or ALT >= 3x ULN and ALP < 2x ULN (DILI)

alp.note: DILI criterion: ALP < 2x ULN required for DILI ECI classification

inclusion: Patient must sign informed consent for tumor testing prior to registration (Step 1)
All criteria listed in protocol section 3.1 must be fulfilled
PD-L1 expression test result must be available at randomization
Registration only accepted from authorized investigators
exclusion: Patients not fulfilling all criteria in protocol section 3.1 are not eligible for registration
Patients not eligible for registration will not be assigned a patient identifier
Registration/randomization from centers not on the authorization list will not be accepted

medication_guidelines.study_medication: Pembrolizumab (anti-PD-1) administered intravenously; blinded vs placebo in Part 1. In Part 2 (after first recurrence and unblinding), cross-over or re-challenge with pembrolizumab. Treatment duration up to 1 year in Part 1 (with possible stop at 1 year at investigator discretion for local recurrence). Imaging follow-up every 12 weeks in Part 2.
prohibited: New anticancer treatment initiated during blinded phase may affect censoring of RFS endpoint
Proxy completion of QoL questionnaires is not allowed unless specifically permitted by the protocol
allowed: Corticosteroids (e.g., prednisone 1-2 mg/kg/day or equivalent) for management of immune-related ECIs as per protocol guidelines
Topical glucocorticosteroids (e.g., betamethasone 0.1% cream, hydrocortisone 1%) for Grade 2 skin ECIs
Oral anti-pruritics (e.g., diphenhydramine HCl, hydroxyzine HCl) for skin ECIs
IV fluids, antihistamines, NSAIDs, acetaminophen, narcotics, oxygen, pressors, epinephrine for infusion reactions
IVIG or other immunosuppressive therapies per local guidelines for Grade 3-4 neurologic ECIs
Radiotherapy allowed before Part 2 per protocol (as noted in v7.0 changes)
Investigator's choice of treatment for patients not receiving pembrolizumab in Part 2 (SARs to be reported to MAH)
